core_mechanism: a master equation for a network of coupled two-state (binary) stochastic jump processes with non-reciprocal (asymmetric) and time-delayed coupling, where the delay makes the dynamics non-Markovian and breaks detailed balance, producing a non-equilibrium steady state whose stationary distribution flattens toward uniform occupancy while sustained oscillatory temporal correlations persist
brief_reason: The abstract models collective dynamics of stochastic binary units whose transition rates depend on other units' past states through asymmetric, finite-propagation-time couplings, yielding delay-induced uniform steady-state probabilities coexisting with anti-symmetry-driven oscillatory temporal correlations distinct from equilibrium behavior.
